title: SDL PlatformManifest
description: A PlatformManifest is the top-level Container at layer_platform. It describes the whole platform — all services visible as actors, the business domains they belong to, and the shared infrastructure they run on. One platform manifest per system (or per logical platform boundary). Typically generated by the synthesis tool from reading all service manifests, then human-edited for domain and infrastructure metadata.

perspectives.name: business
perspectives.description: What the platform does — its purpose, the domains it covers, the key actors (services) involved. Written in plain language for architects and new team members.

perspectives.name: topology
perspectives.description: How the platform is deployed — shared infrastructure, network boundaries, team ownership. Technical context for engineers and ops.

dl.notes: PlatformManifest = Container (the platform is the outermost Container in the SDL zoom chain). Each entry in services[] is a ServiceActor — a child Container representing a service at platform scope. PlatformConnect objects (inter-Container Connects) are materialized separately by the synthesis tool.

dlVersion.description: The SDL spec version this file conforms to.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this platform.

description.description: One paragraph: what business capability this platform delivers, what domains it covers, who its primary users are.

domains.description: Business domains covered by this platform. Aligns with DDD bounded contexts.

services.description: The services that make up this platform, each described as a ServiceActor — the platform-level view of that service.

id.description: Matches the 'id' in the service's own manifest.sdl.json. Used as a reference key by ServiceFlow steps and PlatformConnect objects.

kind.description: The broad category of this actor. Omit for standard backend services. Use 'frontend' for browser or mobile apps, 'external' for third-party systems modelled as platform actors, 'worker' for async/batch processes with no inbound REST interface.

domain.description: Which domain this service belongs to.

sdl_ref.description: Path to this service's manifest.sdl.json, relative to the platform root.

exposes.description: Interfaces this service exposes. Copied from the service manifest — protocol-prefixed notation.

consumes.description: Interfaces this service depends on. Copied from the service manifest.

team.description: Team or squad that owns this service.

shared_infrastructure.description: Infrastructure components shared across services in this platform.

stats.description: Auto-generated summary counts. Populated by tooling.
